description: Official KIVI (K2V2 Int2, group_size=32, asym scale+zero) vs CDA (K4V2+HMMA) decode-attention latency at Llama-3.1-8B GQA shape.
shape: H_q=32 H_kv=8 D=128 (Llama-3.1-8B GQA, group_size=4). Same shape for both methods — apples-to-apples.
kivi_impl: <PATH_REDACTED> kivi_gemv.gemv_forward_cuda_outer_dim via cuda_bmm_fA_qB_outer wrapper. K quant-along-outer-dim (tokens), V quant-along-last-dim (channels). group_size=32 in quantization dim.

kv_memory_per_token.kivi_k2v2_gqa_8heads: K: 8 heads × (2b × 128 D + 2×fp16 × 128/32 groups) = 144 B/tok. V same. Total 288 B/tok.
kv_memory_per_token.cda_k4v2_gqa_8heads: 8 heads × (4b K + 2b V + 8B norms) per slot = 104 B/tok.
kv_memory_per_token.ratio: CDA ≈ 2.77x less KV memory per token vs KIVI K2V2

caveats: KIVI kernel supports GQA (nh/nh_kv separate) — fully fair shape match.
KIVI's full method keeps residual_length=128 most-recent tokens in fp16; we bench pure-quant path, representing dominant cost for long N.

summary.B_1: CDA loses at N=4K (KIVI 0.61×) due to fixed HMMA setup cost; wins 2.35-3.06× at N≥16K.
summary.B_4_to_32: CDA wins 1.89-2.93× across all (B, N) grid — consistent 2-3×.
summary.asymptote: Ratio tracks CDA's ~2.77× memory advantage (KIVI K2V2: 288 B/tok, CDA K4V2: 104 B/tok).
summary.headline: CDA 1.89-3.06× faster than KIVI at B≥4 OR N≥16K; one short-N edge case KIVI wins.
summary.key_narrative: KIVI is the strongest official baseline we have (GQA-native kernel, tight fused gemv). Even so, CDA's compute-on-compressed removes the dequant step and the 4.92× lower memory per token shows up as consistent ~2.5× speedup.
